response.justification: Strong, broad software engineering background (web, mobile, embedded, low-latency systems) with 13+ years of experience and a CS degree, satisfying the bachelor's-plus-experience educational path. However, the role is a Principal-level hardware specialization—SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance optimization, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, and hardware-software co-design—none of which appears in the candidate's history. Their performance work is at the application/microservice layer (DLang async I/O, caching), not silicon. There is no deep-learning, accelerator, or chip-modeling experience, making this a fundamental domain mismatch despite solid general engineering credentials.
strengths: 13+ years of software engineering with low-latency, high-throughput systems experience
Hands-on C/C++ embedded work (LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms, audio synthesis)
Meets the bachelor's-degree + 8 years educational/experience threshold
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or AI-accelerator hardware experience
No deep-learning, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background
Profile is full-stack/web-centric, not principal-level hardware architecture
